content:
A black hole is a place in space where gravity is super strong—so strong that nothing can escape from it, not even light! Imagine if you had a vacuum cleaner so powerful it could suck up everything nearby. That’s kind of like what a black hole does. It forms when a really big star runs out of fuel and collapses, shrinking into a tiny spot with an enormous amount of mass packed inside.

Because the black hole’s gravity is so strong, anything that gets too close gets pulled in and can never come back out. The edge around a black hole is called the event horizon. It’s like a point of no return—once you cross it, you’re stuck inside forever. We can’t see black holes directly because no light can escape them, but scientists know they are there by watching how stars and gas move near these invisible space monsters. Black holes are mysterious and amazing!
